The question asks to compare the percentage of patients without cancer worsening when comparing "treatment for the liver" to "chemotherapy". The articles discuss "SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy" versus "chemotherapy alone". "Treatment for the liver" refers to the SIR-Spheres component, which is used in combination with chemotherapy. Article 1 states that "patients receiving SIR-Spheres had... longer time to disease progression (9.7 vs. 15.9 months)" [1]. This sentence contains an apparent contradiction: if 9.7 months is for SIR-Spheres and 15.9 months is for chemotherapy, then SIR-Spheres has a *shorter* time to progression, which contradicts the claim of "longer time to disease progression". However, the article's overall conclusion is that "Treatment with SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy is more effective than chemotherapy alone" [1]. To resolve this internal inconsistency and align with the overall conclusion of effectiveness, it is reasonable to interpret the numbers as (chemotherapy alone vs. SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy), meaning 9.7 months for chemotherapy alone and 15.9 months for SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy. Under this interpretation, SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy indeed leads to a longer time to disease progression.

Article 2 clearly states that the combination therapy showed "longer time to progression (18.6 vs. 3.6 months)" [2]. Here, 18.6 months is for SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy, and 3.6 months is for chemotherapy alone, indicating a significantly longer time to progression for the combination therapy.

Both articles, when interpreted consistently, indicate that SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy results in a longer time to disease progression compared to chemotherapy alone. A longer time to disease progression means a higher percentage of patients without cancer worsening.

**Full Answer**: The percentage of patients without cancer worsening is higher when comparing treatment for the liver (SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy) to chemotherapy alone. This is because SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy leads to a longer time to disease progression. In one trial, the time to disease progression was 15.9 months for SIR-Spheres plus chemotherapy compared to 9.7 months for chemotherapy alone [1].
